title: Colouring Reconfiguration Complexity Classification (Feghali et al.)
statement_text: Determine the complexity of finding a path in the vertex colouring reconfiguration graph $R_\ell(G)$ between two given $\ell$-colourings of a graph $G$ of maximum degree $\Delta$, for all pairs $(\ell, \Delta)$.
context_text: The $k$-colouring reconfiguration problem is PSPACE-hard even if $k=4$ and $G$ is planar bipartite. Feghali et al. [JGT, 2016] initiated the complexity classification for graphs of maximum degree $\Delta$, leaving exactly one open case $(k,\Delta)$. Resolving that case requires finding in polynomial time a partition $(A,B)$ of the vertex set of a graph of maximum degree $k$ such that $A$ is an independent set and $B$ induces a $(k-2)$-degenerate graph.
paper_contribution: Provides O(n)-time (for $k=1$) and O(n^2)-time (for $k\geq 2$) algorithms for finding $k$-degenerate decompositions of graphs of maximum degree $k$, thereby resolving the remaining open case and completing the complexity classification.
notes: Reference [24] in the paper is cited as 'Feghali et al. [JGT, 2016]'; the exact values of the remaining open case $(k,\Delta)$ are not spelled out in the available text excerpt.
paper_title: Recognizing Graphs Close to Bipartite Graphs with an Application to Colouring Reconfiguration
